recentArticles.title: Kehlani releases highly-anticipated self-titled album - The AU Review
recentArticles.snippet: # Kehlani releases highly-anticipated self-titled album. **Kehlani** marks her birthday with the release of her fifth studio album, ***KEHLANI***, a project that feels deliberately stripped back to the essence of who she is as an artist. Self-titled albums often carry that weight of identity, and here it comes through in the emotional clarity of the songwriting – intimate, reflective, and unafraid to sit in discomfort. The album doesn’t reach outward so much as it settles inward, offering a clearer, more grounded reflection of an artist who knows exactly what she wants to say – and how she wants it to feel. * John Vincent III releases his much anticipated sophomore album, Songs…. * Tusks releases new single "Adore", and announces new album GOLD. * AYLA announces debut album Dopamine and releases a single to celebrate. * Georgia Lines releases her stunning debut album, The Rose of Jericho. * Ruel releases third single from his upcoming album Kicking My Feet.

The development of that track, as Kehlani recently explained to Complex, proved instrumental in a period of artistic growth reflected on the new album. Elsewhere in her recent Complex interview, Kehlani also looked back on how impactful Nick Cannon’s early support was for her journey as an artist, calling him a “great guy.” In fact, according to her, there are actually multiple artists who have similar stories about the *Wild ’n Out* creator and *All That* alum. See the full interview, also featuring Kehlani’s thoughts on AI and what it felt like to take home two Grammys this year for “Folded,” below. recentArticles.title: ‘Michael’ Heading Toward $85 Million-Plus Opening Weekend At Box Office - Yahoo News UK
recentArticles.snippet: # ‘Michael’ Heading Toward $85 Million-Plus Opening Weekend At Box Office. The Michael Jackson biopic *Michael* is projected to earn more than $85 million in its opening weekend at the domestic box office. *Michael*, which stars Jaafar Jackson (Michael Jackson’s nephew/Jermaine Jackson’s son) in the title role, officially opened in North American theaters on Friday after special Wednesday night fan screenings and Thursday previews. As such, if a projection for its opening weekend by *Deadline* published on Friday night is any indication, Michael is taking a big first step toward its aspirations to make a sequel a reality. Lionsgate announced in an email Friday that *Michael* earned $3.8 million at fan screenings Wednesday night before playing in previews Thursday night, which resulted in a two-day domestic total of $12.6 million. Lionsgate also announced Friday that *Michael* also opened with $18.5 million in ticket sales in 82 international markets on Wednesday.

recentArticles.title: OKP’s Biggest Takeaways From Kehlani’s Self-Titled New Album - Okayplayer
recentArticles.snippet: Listening to Kehlani’s latest feels like my mom’s driving me to the first day of 7th grade 22 years ago, and from the jump, it’s clear that that’s the point. The retro R&B aesthetic feels more like a tool rather than an endgame, with her tender, yet sultry vocals and introspection making the production feel inhabited instead of “used.” Crash, on the other hand, felt scattershot, with Kehlani shifting between sonics with a clumsiness that was kind of unbefitting of her vocal talent and songwriting. * #### The Listening: New Music From Kehlani, Reason, Fredo Bang & BigXThaPlug Latto & GloRilla and More. missy elliott kehlani brandy big sean new music t-pain usher. * #### The Listening: New Music From Kehlani, Reason, Fredo Bang & BigXThaPlug Latto & GloRilla and More. missy elliott kehlani brandy big sean new music news t-pain usher. * #### The Listening: New Music From Kehlani, Reason, Fredo Bang & BigXThaPlug Latto & GloRilla and More.

recentArticles.title: Jada Pinkett Smith asks court to make Will Smith’s former friend pay her $49,000 legal bills - Los Angeles Times
recentArticles.snippet: * Bilaal Salaam sued after airing podcast allegations about the Smiths’ private lives, which Pinkett Smith characterized as a “money shakedown” and part of a broader attempt to extort her. Jada Pinkett Smith is asking a judge to make Bilaal Salaam cover the $49,000 in legal fees she racked up fighting claims he made in a December lawsuit. According to a motion filed April 20 and obtained by The Times, Pinkett Smith is asking that Salaam pay $49,181.23, consisting of “reasonable attorneys’ fees incurred” in connection with Pinkett Smith’s successful special motion to strike Salaam’s complaint, “plus further fees and costs associated with this motion.”. Salaam’s suit claims that Pinkett Smith told him he would “end up missing or catch a bullet” if he kept “telling her personal business.” She also allegedly pressured him to sign a non-disclosure agreement. Pinkett Smith swiftly responded during an appearance on “The Breakfast Club” and said that Salaam started the rumors as part of a broader “money shakedown” and that his claims were “ridiculous and nonsense.”.

And so I started making poor decisions," Jay said. "The next night I made a plan of how to do it, and I did it," Jay remembered. Jay made a plan to end his life. He said yes, and listened as the man began to pray. "I wish I could tell you that I remember the [exact] words that he said to me, but what I remember is that his words landed with me, and instead of wanting my life to be over, suddenly I saw hope," Jay said. The interaction happened nearly ten years ago, but it was a pivotal moment in Jay's life, and one he thinks about all the time. Jay says that he wishes he could meet that man again and express his appreciation.

recentArticles.snippet: # Nikki Glaser Roasts Katy Perry, Hailey Bieber, Victoria Beckham and More at Time 100 Gala. Fresh off hosting the last two Golden Globe Awards, the comedian didn't hold back at this year's event honoring the magazine's picks for the world's most influential people. Glaser hosted the 20th annual Time 100 Gala on Thursday night at Jazz at Lincoln Center in New York City, which celebrates *Time*‘s list of the world’s most influential people. And in true Glaser style, she made it a roast, playfully taking jabs at Katy Perry, Hailey Bieber, Victoria Beckham, MrBeast, Kate Hudson and more, as well as *Time* itself. “Welcome to the Time 100, the one list you actually want to be on this year,” the comedian said to a star-studded crowd kicking off the event. “One of the most impressive people on the list is Victoria Beckham, of course. ### How an 18-Year-Old TikToker’s Scientology “Run” Inspired a Trend — and Why He Says It’s Gone Too Far. recentArticles.title: Kehlani’s self-titled album tracks ranked from worst to best - Rolling Out
recentArticles.snippet: # Kehlani’s self-titled album tracks ranked from worst to best. Oakland R&B star blends nostalgia, vocals, and legacy collabs in a genre-shaping album. Kehlani’s self-titled album arrives after a decade of steady growth from mixtape standout to mainstream R&B force. The album draws heavily from 1990s and 2000s R&B, not as imitation but as reference point. Jimmy Jam and Terry Lewis contribute to the album’s more classic R&B textures, while other tracks lean into contemporary polish. Big Sean)** – Smooth but relatively understated mid-album moment. Usher)** – Classic R&B breakup exchange with polished delivery. Cardi B)** – Lively collaboration with strong mainstream appeal. Folded** – The project’s defining hit and emotional anchor. Unlearn** – Gospel-leaning closer that ties the album’s emotional themes together. By the final track, Kehlani reads less like a diary and more like a reflection on R&B itself. * album, charts, Collaboration, Folded, Kehlani, Music, Pop, ranking, review, rnb.

recentArticles.title: Review: Dancing around controversy, the empathetic 'Michael' bows out before getting dark - Los Angeles Times
recentArticles.snippet: Jaafar Jackson in the movie “Michael.”. (Meanwhile, there’s no Janet, not even onscreen in the background or mentioned at all.) The Jacksons’ biological and, presumably, financial ties to the project would raise a World Cup stadium of red flags even without Jaafar Jackson, Michael’s nephew, playing the lead. But suffocating nepotism might be the only way for now to capture someone as singularly strange as Jackson — if Jaafar delivered a line reading that wasn’t note perfect, it’s likely that someone in his clan would pipe up and say so. Jackson’s music videos — “short films,” he preferred to call them — did that too, in abbreviation. After adult Michael is ordered to be the central cog in the Jacksons’ post-”Thriller” Victory tour, the film cuts to him on the couch watching his idol Charlie Chaplin work the assembly line in “Modern Times.” Alongside that, we catch glimpses of the megastar’s effect on his siblings, like concert scenes where the spotlight is only on him, or in Tito’s choice to wear a bedazzled hat with his name in all-caps.

Critically, this investment also allows the team at Futures to begin building out the next phase of the company, which includes expanding into the catalog space and development of our own internal technologies to help artists maximize value and fan experience across their releases,” added Wallace. recentArticles.title: Sitting in a jail cell, alone and hopeless, a man's life is suddenly changed - capradio.org
recentArticles.snippet: # Sitting in a jail cell, alone and hopeless, a man's life is suddenly changed. Jay (not pictured) found himself alone and hopeless in a jail cell when a fellow inmate's unexpected words of comfort changed his life. And so I started making poor decisions," Jay said. Jay made a plan to end his life. "I wish I could tell you that I remember the [exact] words that he said to me, but what I remember is that his words landed with me, and instead of wanting my life to be over, suddenly I saw hope," Jay said. The interaction happened nearly ten years ago, but it was a pivotal moment in Jay's life, and one he thinks about all the time. Jay says that he wishes he could meet that man again and express his appreciation. To share the story of your unsung hero with the Hidden Brain team, record a voice memo on your phone and send it to myunsunghero@hiddenbrain.org.*.
recentArticles.summary: Sitting in a jail cell, alone and hopeless, a man's life is suddenly changed - capradio.org

recentArticles.title: Time 100 Gala 2026 red carpet: Nikki Glaser, Hilary Duff, Dakota Johnson, and more - Page Six
recentArticles.snippet: # Time 100 Gala 2026 red carpet: Hailey Bieber, Hilary Duff, Dakota Johnson, and more. Published  April 23, 2026, 7:51 p.m. ET. The stars are out in full force as the world’s most influential figures gather for the annual Time100 Gala, celebrating this year’s TIME100 list of leaders, innovators, and icons. Held in New York City, the evening brought together celebrities, global change makers, and cultural powerhouses. See all the looks and arrivals in the gallery below. ## Victoria and David Beckham don matching blue outfits for NYC dinner and more star snaps.

recentArticles.title: Teddy Swims Talks Coachella, White Claw Collab And Friends Who Matter - Forbes
recentArticles.snippet: He brought out Joe Jonas for a duet on the Jonas Brothers’ 2007 single “When You Look Me in the Eyes.” He welcomed Vanessa Carlton to the stage to sing — and accompany him on piano for — her 2002 smash “A Thousand Miles.” And there was legendary Van Halen frontman David Lee Roth, striding out to deliver their ’80s classic “Jump.” By Weekend 2, Roth was back for more and, in a full-circle gesture, Swims returned the favor on the final day of Coachella by popping on stage to perform “Lose Control” alongside fellow soul singer and past collaborator Givēon. “I love that White Claw also has this burden for real connection, bringing people together and it feels like we’re so aligned on just how do we keep things intimate and loving, caring about one another and being real connective,” Swims, whose favorite White Claw flavor is Ruby Grapefruit, says of the partnership.
recentArticles.summary: Teddy Swims Talks Coachella, White Claw Collab And Friends Who Matter - Forbes
